prerequisites: A reachable MS SQL Server instance (SQL Server 2016 or greater, or Azure SQL Database). Provide its host and port.
The target database name and the schema tables are written to (commonly dbo).
A SQL Server login (user) with permission to create and write tables in the target schema, such as CREATE TABLE, ALTER, INSERT, and SELECT on the target schema.
Network access from the destination to the database. If the server sits in a private network or VPC, allow the connecting IP or use the built-in SSH tunnel.

For bulk load only: an Azure Blob Storage account (account name and container) plus either a storage account key or a shared access signature (SAS) token, and an external data source configured in SQL Server for BULK INSERT.

authMethods.name: SQL Server authentication (username and password)
authMethods.summary: Connect with a SQL Server login. Set the user field and supply its password in the password secret field. SQL Server supports SQL Server authentication (login plus password) in addition to Windows authentication; this connector uses the login-and-password method.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel credentials (optional)
authMethods.summary: When tunnel_method is set to an SSH tunnel, authenticate to the bastion host either with an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or with a password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password). Used to reach a database that is not directly reachable.

authMethods.name: Azure Blob Storage credentials (bulk load only)
authMethods.summary: When load_type is the bulk (Azure Blob Storage) option, provide either a storage account key (load_type.azure_blob_storage_account_key) or a shared access signature token (load_type.shared_access_signature) so the connector can stage data in Blob Storage before running BULK INSERT.

authMethods.name: TLS trust store password (optional)
authMethods.summary: When ssl_method uses certificate verification with a custom trust store, supply the trust store password (ssl_method.trustStorePassword) used by the JDBC driver to open the encrypted connection.

setupSteps.title: Create a dedicated SQL Server login
setupSteps.body: In SQL Server, create a login and database user for the connector. Grant it the permissions needed to write the synced data, for example CREATE TABLE and ALTER on the target schema plus INSERT and SELECT. Use the login name as the user value and its password as the password secret.

setupSteps.title: Enter connection details
setupSteps.body: Set host and port for the MSSQL server, database for the target database, and schema for the schema tables are written to (the usual value is dbo). Optionally add extra JDBC settings via jdbc_url_params.

setupSteps.title: Choose the load type
setupSteps.body: Set load_type to Insert to load rows with SQL INSERT statements, or to the Azure Blob Storage bulk option to stage data in Blob Storage and load it with BULK INSERT. For bulk load, provide the Azure Blob Storage account and container, supply either load_type.azure_blob_storage_account_key or load_type.shared_access_signature, and reference the external data source configured in SQL Server.

setupSteps.title: Configure SSL/TLS
setupSteps.body: Set ssl_method to Unencrypted, Encrypted (trust server certificate) to use TLS without verifying the certificate, or Encrypted (verify certificate) to verify the server certificate. When verifying with a custom trust store, set ssl_method.trustStorePassword.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel if needed
setupSteps.body: If the database is not directly reachable, set tunnel_method to an SSH tunnel and authenticate to the bastion host with either tunnel_method.ssh_key or tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password. Leave tunnel_method unset for a direct connection.
